title: Movement in the early republic
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on movement in the early republic

assessment_items.question_content:
>“In examining the question how the disturbances on the frontiers are to be quieted, two modes present themselves, by which the object might perhaps be effected; the first of which is by raising an army, and [destroying the resisting] tribes entirely, or [secondly] by forming treaties of peace with them, in which their rights and limits should be explicitly defined, and the treaties observed on the part of the United States with the most rigid justice, by punishing the whites, who should violate the same. . . . The Indians being the prior occupants, possess the right of the soil. It cannot be taken from them unless by their free consent, or by the right of conquest in case of a just war. To disposses them on any other principle, would be a gross violation of the fundamental laws of nature, and of that distributive justice which is the glory of a nation.”

>-Source: Henry Knox, to President George Washington, 1789

**The ideas in the excerpt most directly contributed to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: violent conflicts between white settlers and Indigenous communities over removing Indigenous peoples from western lands
diplomatic resolutions between Indigenous communities and the United States, placing American settlers on reserved land
laws passed by the United States that limited westward expansion to the east of the Mississippi River
laws that gave Indigenous peoples full citizenship status under the United States Constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
>“In examining the question how the disturbances on the frontiers are to be quieted, two modes present themselves, by which the object might perhaps be effected; the first of which is by raising an army, and [destroying the resisting] tribes entirely, or [secondly] by forming treaties of peace with them, in which their rights and limits should be explicitly defined, and the treaties observed on the part of the United States with the most rigid justice, by punishing the whites, who should violate the same. . . . The Indians being the prior occupants, possess the right of the soil. It cannot be taken from them unless by their free consent, or by the right of conquest in case of a just war. To disposses them on any other principle, would be a gross violation of the fundamental laws of nature, and of that distributive justice which is the glory of a nation.”

>-Source: Henry Knox, to President George Washington, 1789

**Which of the following events best represents a continuity of the sentiments expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The passage of the Treaty of Greenville in 1795 and the American acquisition of Ohio and part of Indiana
The Missouri Compromise of 1820, which created new regulations over the expansion of slavery to new states
The ratification of the 14th Amendment in 1863 and the extension of citizenship to any person born in the United States
The increased support for assimilationist policies to force Indigenous peoples and immigrants to adopt American culture  in the 1880s and 1890s

assessment_items.question_content:
>“In examining the question how the disturbances on the frontiers are to be quieted, two modes present themselves, by which the object might perhaps be effected; the first of which is by raising an army, and [destroying the resisting] tribes entirely, or [secondly] by forming treaties of peace with them, in which their rights and limits should be explicitly defined, and the treaties observed on the part of the United States with the most rigid justice, by punishing the whites, who should violate the same. . . . The Indians being the prior occupants, possess the right of the soil. It cannot be taken from them unless by their free consent, or by the right of conquest in case of a just war. To disposses them on any other principle, would be a gross violation of the fundamental laws of nature, and of that distributive justice which is the glory of a nation.”

>-Source: Henry Knox, to President George Washington, 1789

**The excerpt most directly reflects tribal efforts to do which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: maintain control of tribal lands and limit migration of white settlers
gain access to new land in the Northwest Territory after the American Revolution
retain control of the Mississippi River to continue trade with European allies
obtain an amendment that would give Indigenous individuals US citizenship

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/12217b778cc1a1597d5234f972e6089d.jpg)

>-Source: R.F. Zogbaum, [*The Battle of Fallen Timbers,*](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fallen_timbers.jpg) *Harper's Magazine,* 1896

**The image most closely reflects which of the following developments in the political climate in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased efforts by the United States to gain control of Indigenous lands
renewed efforts by the United States to end conflicts with Indigenous communities through diplomacy
extension of citizenship rights to Indigenous individuals to protect Indigenous communities from white settlers
decreased access to land for white settlement after several diplomatic treaties with Indigenous nations

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/12217b778cc1a1597d5234f972e6089d.jpg)

>-Source: R.F. Zogbaum, [*The Battle of Fallen Timbers,*](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fallen_timbers.jpg) *Harper's Magazine,* 1896

**Which of the following most directly led to the circumstances illustrated by the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased efforts by Indigenous nations to limit migration of white settlers
increased efforts by Indigenous nations to gain sovereignty in the American government
decreased efforts by white settlers to expand westward into Indigenous lands for fear of military conflict
decreased efforts by the United States to protect white settlers from conflict with Indigenous nations

assessment_items.question_content:
>"I have long been of Opinion that a well-conducted western Colony, if it could be settled with the Approbation of the Indians would be of great National Advantage with respect to the Trade, and particularly useful to the old Colonies as a Security to their Frontiers. I am glad to find that you, whose Knowledge of Indian Affairs and the Temper of those People far exceeds mine, entertain the same Sentiments, and think such an Establishment in the Ilinoias [sic] Country practicable. . . .

>"It grieves me to hear that our Frontier People are yet greater Barbarians than the Indians, and continue to murder them in time of Peace. I hope your Negociations [sic] will prevent a new War, which those Murders give great Reason to apprehend; and that the several Governments will find some Method of preventing such horrid Outrages for the future." 

>-Source: Benjamin Franklin, [In a letter to Sir William Johnson](https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Franklin/01-13-02-0146), 1766

**The ideas of Benjamin Franklin, as expressed in the excerpt, had most in common with the ideas of which of the following groups?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Quakers of Pennsylvania
The Paxton Boys
The Democratic-Republicans
The Anti-Federalists

assessment_items.question_content:
>"I have long been of Opinion that a well-conducted western Colony, if it could be settled with the Approbation of the Indians would be of great National Advantage with respect to the Trade, and particularly useful to the old Colonies as a Security to their Frontiers. I am glad to find that you, whose Knowledge of Indian Affairs and the Temper of those People far exceeds mine, entertain the same Sentiments, and think such an Establishment in the Ilinoias [sic] Country practicable. . . .

>"It grieves me to hear that our Frontier People are yet greater Barbarians than the Indians, and continue to murder them in time of Peace. I hope your Negociations [sic] will prevent a new War, which those Murders give great Reason to apprehend; and that the several Governments will find some Method of preventing such horrid Outrages for the future." 

>-Source: Benjamin Franklin, [In a letter to Sir William Johnson](https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Franklin/01-13-02-0146), 1766

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased violence between white frontiersmen and Indigenous nations
increased interracial marriages between Indigenous peoples
increased conflict with British forces in the Northwest Territory
increased debates about the sovereignty of Indigenous nations

assessment_items.question_content:
>"I have long been of Opinion that a well-conducted western Colony, if it could be settled with the Approbation of the Indians would be of great National Advantage with respect to the Trade, and particularly useful to the old Colonies as a Security to their Frontiers. I am glad to find that you, whose Knowledge of Indian Affairs and the Temper of those People far exceeds mine, entertain the same Sentiments, and think such an Establishment in the Ilinoias [sic] Country practicable. . . .

>"It grieves me to hear that our Frontier People are yet greater Barbarians than the Indians, and continue to murder them in time of Peace. I hope your Negociations [sic] will prevent a new War, which those Murders give great Reason to apprehend; and that the several Governments will find some Method of preventing such horrid Outrages for the future." 

>-Source: Benjamin Franklin, [In a letter to Sir William Johnson](https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Franklin/01-13-02-0146), 1766

**Based on the excerpt, which of the following issues of the period was Benjamin Franklin most likely concerned with?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increasing conflicts between settlers in the Northwest Territory and Natives
increasing sectional conflict between the North and the South over the expansion of slavery
increasing conflicts between the states and the national government over the issue of the constitutionality of a national bank
increasing conflicts between the United States and foreign countries over boundary divisions between territories

assessment_items.question_content:
US region | Number of enslaved Africans, 1790 | Number of enslaved Africans, 1800
- | - | -
New England | 3,763 | 1,339
Middle states | 45,210 | 41,184
Southern states | 648,651 | 847,281

>-Source: [United States Census](https://www2.census.gov/prod2/statcomp/documents/HistoricalStatisticsoftheUnitedStates1789-1945.pdf)

**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1790 to 1800 shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a rise in  anti-slavery sentiment in Northern states
the end of the Atlantic Slave Trade in 1808
the expansion of slavery into the Ohio River Valley
a federal ban on slavery enacted in the Three-Fifths Compromise

assessment_items.question_content:
US region | Number of enslaved Africans, 1790 | Number of enslaved Africans, 1800
- | - | -
New England | 3,763 | 1,339
Middle states | 45,210 | 41,184
Southern states | 648,651 | 847,281

>-Source: [United States Census](https://www2.census.gov/prod2/statcomp/documents/HistoricalStatisticsoftheUnitedStates1789-1945.pdf)

**Which of the following was an important effect of the processes reflected in the diagram?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased sectionalism across the United States
increased partisanship in local and state politics
reduced anti-slavery sentiment in the New England states
reduced westward movement past the Appalachian mountains

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Enslavement of persons of African descent by state, 1790** ||
State | Total Population | Percent enslaved  | Number of enslaved persons
- | - | - | -
Massachusetts | $378,717$ | --- | ---
Connecticut| $238,141$ | $1.16\%$ | $2,763$
New York | $340,120$ | $6.27\%$ | $21,326$
Pennsylvania | $434,373$ | $0.86\%$| $3,736$
Virginia  | $748,308$ | $39.21\%$ | $293,412$
North Carolina | $393,751$ | $25.55\%$ | $100,604$
South Carolina | $249,973$ | $43.00\%$ | $107,489$
Georgia | $82,5484$ | $35.45\%$ | $29,264$


>-Source: [US Census](https://www2.census.gov/library/publications/decennial/1850/1850a/1850a-13.pdf)

**The difference in slave populations depicted in the graph was most directly a result of which of the following factors?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A difference in the dependence on enslaved labor to produce major cash crops
a difference in political enfranchisement of formerly enslaved persons of African descent
a difference in available public education in the North and the South
a difference in federal laws about the use of enslaved labor in certain states

**The trend depicted in the chart most directly contributed to which of the following developments after 1800?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of distinct regional attitudes toward slavery
the end to the African slave trade in 1808
the rise of the Republican party as the defenders of slavery in the South
the end of a farm-based economy in the American frontier
